parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary command execution as the application OS user.
parsed_findings.proof: JSON like `{"name":"a;malicious#","src":"/var/data/app"}` yields a command where `;` terminates `tar` and runs trailing shell syntax.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send POST `/backup` with valid `src` and a `name` containing shell metacharacters, then observe unintended command effects.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized `name` is embedded in a string passed to `os.system`, enabling shell metacharacter injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Traversal bypass plus archive retrieval completes impact.
parsed_findings.impact: Sensitive host files may be packed into `/tmp/{name}.tgz` and exfiltrated.
parsed_findings.proof: The string `/var/data/../../etc/passwd` begins with `/var/data/` yet resolves outside `/var/data/` for filesystem operations.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST `/backup` with `src` set to `/var/data/../../etc/passwd` and inspect the resulting tarball contents.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: `startswith('/var/data/')` accepts traversal sequences such as `/var/data/../../etc/passwd`, so `tar` can archive outside the intended tree.
